name: The Farmers State Bank Of Blue Mound
description_short: Community bank serving Kansas since 1913 with FDIC insurance, multiple branches, and online banking. Privately held with 100+ years of local service.
description_long:
The Farmers State Bank of Blue Mound was chartered in August 1913 by 26 area farmers with $10,000 in capital stock. The bank has operated continuously for over 110 years, serving Linn and Anderson counties in Kansas. In 1975, the original stockholders (mostly descendants of the founders) sold the bank to a private party, and it has remained privately held since then. The bank expanded its geographic footprint by acquiring the Bank of Pleasanton in 1989 and Garnett Savings and Loan in 1990, creating a three-branch network across rural Kansas.

The Farmers State Bank of Blue Mound offers standard retail banking services including checking and savings accounts, online banking access, debit cards, ATM services, loan products, and check reordering. The bank maintains three physical branch locations in Blue Mound, Pleasanton, and Garnett, with varying lobby and drive-up hours. They provide FDIC insurance protection backed by the U.S. Government, secured online banking, and customer service via phone and email. The bank also offers loan calculators and financial education resources.

What distinguishes this institution is its deep community roots and stability as a privately held, independent bank operating for over a century in the same rural Kansas region. The bank's historical significance (including surviving bank robberies in 1935, 1937, and 1977) demonstrates resilience. The current rating of 5 stars by Bauer (awarded June 2025) reflects recent performance metrics. The bank maintains traditional business hours and personal customer service rather than 24/7 digital-only operations, suggesting a focus on relationship banking.

Honestly assessed, this is a small-town community bank with limited geographic reach, operating only in Kansas with three branches. Technology appears basic compared to modern fintech or larger institutions—the website emphasizes security warnings about email and encourages customers to call or visit in person for sensitive matters. Hours are restrictive (lobby closes at 3:30 pm in Blue Mound, closed Saturdays everywhere). As a small institution, they likely cannot compete on rates or product innovation with larger banks. However, for customers in their service areas prioritizing local relationships and stability, this bank offers legitimate value.
services: Online banking with secure login and account management
Debit cards with lost/stolen card reporting
ATM access and ATM locator tool
Loan products (specific types not detailed on website)
Check reordering and check cashing services
Wire transfers and bill payment capabilities
Secured email communication via online banking
Customer service via phone and email
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government guarantee
Operating continuously since 1913 with proven stability and local reputation
Privately held since 1975, maintaining independent decision-making
Three convenient branch locations across Kansas with drive-up windows
Online banking and digital account access available
Personal customer service with direct phone lines to branches
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only three branches in rural Kansas (Blue Mound, Pleasanton, Garnett)
Restrictive branch hours (Blue Mound lobby closes at 3:30 pm; all branches closed Saturdays)
Small institution likely unable to compete on rates or product depth with larger regional/national banks
Basic technology infrastructure—website discourages email for sensitive matters and emphasizes phone/in-person contact
No indication of 24/7 online account management or mobile app capabilities

contact.address: 205 South Fifth Street, Blue Mound, KS 66010

best_for: Rural Kansas residents in Linn and Anderson counties who value local banking relationships
Customers prioritizing community-focused banks with deep historical roots over modern digital convenience
Small business owners and farmers in the bank's service area seeking personalized lending decisions
Individuals seeking FDIC-insured deposit safety with a proven, stable institution
diagnosis: Best for rural Kansas residents in Linn and Anderson counties seeking stable, relationship-based community banking with FDIC insurance and personal service. Main caveat: Limited to three branches in small towns with restrictive hours, basic digital infrastructure, and no competitive advantage for rates or product selection versus larger institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 205 South Fifth Street, Blue Mound, KS 66010
